a.label: Boolean flag

b.label: AbortController

slides.title: B wins. Always.

verdicts.title: Flag doesn't cancel anything
verdicts.text: The request still completes — you just ignore the result. Bandwidth wasted, race conditions possible.

verdicts.title: AbortController kills it
verdicts.text: Terminates the fetch at the network level. The request is actually cancelled, not just ignored.

slides.takeaway: AbortController cancels at the network level. The flag pattern is a lie.

slides.title: It cancels everything.
slides.sub: Not just fetch — event listeners, streams, any async operation.

items.text: ctrl.abort() — cancel everything on this signal

slides.teaser: Next: The API that replaces setTimeout forever

caption:
Stop using boolean flags to "cancel" fetch requests. They don't actually cancel anything.

AbortController kills the request at the network level. One signal controls fetch, event listeners, streams — everything.

Which approach do you use? A or B?
